preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 54%, a modest advantage in a match where the underdog Egypt holds a genuine 17% path to victory. The draw emerges as a substantial outcome at 29%, suggesting the contest will be genuinely competitive rather than one-sided.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a relatively tight tactical landscape. Belgium's edge is clear but not commanding; Egypt's 17% win probability is large enough to make them a credible threat. A draw sits between the two outright results and carries nearly the weight of an Egypt victory, indicating that play could plausibly remain level through regulation.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes. Belgium's win probability differs by 4 percentage points, the draw by 4 points, and the Egypt win by under 1 point. Polymarket pricing tracks similarly. This convergence suggests the market has reached a stable read on the matchup that the statistical forecast corroborates.
